title: Dortmanna schimperi
intro: Dortmanna schimperi, also known as African Dodonaea, is a plant species native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, characterized by its thorny shrub-like growth and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna schimperi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Dortmanna schimperi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna. Dortmanna schimperi is native to Ethiopia.
